refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, verifying that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the target specified by the customer.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination
Adjust equipment operational parameters to meet cycle time
Implement real-time monitoring and adaptive control of load phase
Enhance operator training and procedural adherence for load phase efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination

potential_sources: Interdependence between load phase and travel/dump phases timing
Influence of external site conditions on cycle time
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on the load phase process itself, aiming to isolate and optimize it independently from other phases, supporting decoupling and modularity.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time can be influenced primarily by load phase process improvements
External factors such as site conditions and safety policies remain constant or are managed separately
Operator behavior and training impact cycle time but are outside this DP scope
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive control or monitoring DPs if real-time adjustments are critical to meeting cycle time targets under variable conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing and optimizing operational processes including load phase sequencing and coordination

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational data and sends status updates related to load phase optimization

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends commands and coordination tasks to other modules or subsystems during load phase

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems and modules to coordinate load phase activities

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as load phase optimization is a core operational function; minimal diffusion as coordination mostly internal
Vehicle module is more about physical control and status; load phase sequencing would require significant external coordination, causing diffusion
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; load phase optimization is broader and would diffuse across other modules
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; load phase optimization is outside primary scope, causing high diffusion

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' because it directly manages operational processes and can internally coordinate sequencing with minimal external diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints affect sequencing
Dependency on accurate telemetry data which may cause coupling with external systems
Coordination with Safety Services for safe sequencing may cause some interface complexity

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase by focusing on process sequencing and coordination, which are key factors influencing cycle time. The DP is logically derived and grounded in the acceptance criteria of meeting the p95 cycle time target under specified conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints affect sequencing
Dependency on accurate telemetry data which may cause coupling with external systems
Coordination with Safety Services for safe sequencing may cause some interface complexity
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating it is a suitable and focused boundary for implementing the DP. The diffusion risks are acknowledged but appear manageable given the module's scope and interfaces. The containment hypothesis is well supported by the module containment scores and scope description.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces with telemetry and tasking systems to monitor and control load phase sequencing, enabling measurement of cycle time performance against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces and ownership candidate identified, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface protocols and data requirements with Vehicle and Safety Services modules to mitigate diffusion risks and interface complexity.
Include contingency handling in the NGHE Solution module design for telemetry data inaccuracies to reduce coupling risk.
Consider defining clear boundaries and responsibilities in coordination tasks to avoid overlap with Vehicle module functions related to physical constraints.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical derivation, manageable diffusion risks, and feasible verification. The DP and IM are well aligned with the FR, and the containment hypothesis is supported by cohesion and diffusion scores. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and interface clarity rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level with the noted revisions rather than further decomposing, which could introduce complexity and risk.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface protocols and data requirements with Vehicle and Safety Services modules, include contingency handling for telemetry data inaccuracies, and define clear boundaries in coordination tasks to mitigate diffusion risks and interface complexity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively implement the DP with current scope and interfaces.
Telemetry and tasking systems provide reliable data for monitoring and control within acceptable accuracy limits.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module functions related to physical constraints.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data causing coupling with external systems.
Interface complexity with Safety Services for safe sequencing.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface protocol documentation between NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Safety Services modules.
Contingency handling strategies for telemetry data inaccuracies.
Verification test results demonstrating cycle time performance under specified conditions.
